prompt: Wide architectural three-quarter interior view of a kitchen mid-renovation in a 1980s Methuen MA colonial. Studs are exposed along the back wall, a few sections of wall already drywalled and taped but not painted, raw plywood subfloor underneath, electrical roughs hanging from a junction box with twisted yellow wire nuts. A chalk line snapped along the floor marking the new island footprint. A folded blue tarp in the doorway, a pair of saw horses with a Festool track saw resting on them. Soft cool morning light pours through a bare uncurtained window. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, neutral cool morning light, no hot spots, slightly cool color temperature. A red Milwaukee jobsite radio on the floor as the only saturated color. High-contrast slightly desaturated photography, NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
